name: Pancakes with Nine Fillings- Guchul Pan
recipeIngredient: 10 oz Lean Beef Rump Or Fillet
recipeInstructions:
This delightful dish is an innovative and economical way to serve a do-it-yourself-style appetizer or main-dish. It is usually presented in a special lacquered wood or china tray with the required number of compartments. It is just as effective served on a tray in small bowls or dishes. The pancakes can be made in advance and kept refrigerated until needed, and many of the fillings can also be prepared ahead of time for convenience.
1. Sift the flour into a mixing bowl and make a well in the center.
2. Add the beaten egg, water, milk, salt, white pepper, chili powder, and mix until smooth. Let it rest for 20 minutes.
3. Drain the Chinese mushrooms, reserving the liquid. Discard the stems, then shred the caps finely.
4. Simmer the shredded mushrooms with 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 2 teaspoons sugar, and reserved water in a small pan until softened. Remove from heat and drain well.
5. Peel and cut the carrot into thin strips, then into fine shreds. Heat a small amount of vegetable oil and sesame oil in a pan, stir-fry the carrot until softened but not colored, add salt, pepper, and soy sauce, and remove from heat.
6. Rinse out the pan.
7. Grate the radish and cook similarly to the carrot, omitting soy sauce. Trim green onions, shred finely, and stir-fry briefly in vegetable oil until just softened.
8. Beat egg whites with a pinch of salt and pepper until frothy. Rub an omelet pan or wok with oil and pour in the egg mixture. Cook on low heat, tilting the pan to spread thinly over a wide area. Cook until firm but not colored under, then flip gently. Cook the yolk side briefly.
9. Shred the cooked egg into narrow lengths, then crosswise into short pieces.
10. Thinly shred kim chee and squeeze out excess liquid. Cut beef into paper-thin slices (it helps to partially freeze it first). Heat a little vegetable oil and sesame oil in a pan or wok, stir-fry the beef until colored, add 1 tablespoon soy sauce, sprinkle salt, pepper, and sugar, and splash with extra sesame oil.
11. Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan over moderate heat until golden and popping. Remove, cool slightly, and grind to a fine powder.
12. Mix the dipping sauce ingredients together and divide among several dishes.
13. Use an omelet pan or wok well-oiled to cook pancakes. Pour in batter and cook until the bottom is golden with small bubbles on top. Flip gently and cook the other side briefly. Continue cooking pancakes over medium heat, stacking under a cloth, until all batter is used.
14. Serve pancakes at room temperature with fillings alongside the dipping sauce.
